“A really high quality teacher – was able to speak intelligently on many different issues, and was always happy to discuss and answer questions after class”
“He was the best professor I have had here”
“Professor Mandeville was incredibly helpful and always made the material easy to understand. He explained concepts clearly, provided relatable examples, and kept us engaged throughout every lesson. Not only was he smart and well-prepared, but he also created a friendly and welcoming environment. His sense of humor made class enjoyable, and his willingness to answer questions or offer support truly made a difference in my learning experience.”
“Mr. Mandeville is the GOAT”

title: Policy Work

title: Brian Mandeville | Welcome
text:
Download my CV

I am a PhD candidate in Economics at George Mason University (expected May 2027), where I am also a PhD Fellow at the Mercatus Center and an Adam Smith Fellow. I teach as a Graduate Lecturer at George Mason University and as an Adjunct Lecturer at Catholic University of America.
My research examines the interaction between norms, beliefs, and formal political institutions; the application of Public Choice theory to the nonprofit realm; and the importance of virtue and citizenship writ broadly.
I have found very little to be more rewarding than interacting with my students and helping them understand the benefits of social cooperation that arise as a result of markets and the division of labor.
You can reach me at bwmandeville@gmail.com.
